journal_entry.rationale: Customer previously paid $100 as a deposit, creating a liability (Unearned Revenue or Customer Deposits). Now that the service has been delivered, the obligation is fulfilled: we decrease the liability (debit) and recognize revenue (credit). The debit tuple slot d=1 indicates one liability decrease line; the credit tuple slot c=1 indicates one revenue increase line. No tax is applicable because the deposit was already collected with any applicable tax at the time of receipt; this transaction merely converts unearned to earned revenue.

output.reason: Customer deposit is a liability (unearned revenue). When the service is delivered, we recognize the revenue by decreasing the liability. Liability decrease = debit. This is 1 debit line.

debit_classifier.reason: Customer deposit is a liability (unearned revenue). When the service is delivered, we recognize the revenue by decreasing the liability. Liability decrease = debit. This is 1 debit line.

output.reason: When a service is delivered against a previously received customer deposit, we need to recognize revenue. The customer deposit (unearned revenue liability) is reduced (debited), and service revenue is recognized (credited). The credit side shows: Revenue increase = 1 line.

credit_classifier.reason: When a service is delivered against a previously received customer deposit, we need to recognize revenue. The customer deposit (unearned revenue liability) is reduced (debited), and service revenue is recognized (credited). The credit side shows: Revenue increase = 1 line.
